_comment: This procedure code (SU033A) corresponds to HBP 2.0. HBP 1.0 codes S700055 and S700056 refer to the same procedure description.
procedure_name: DJ stenting including cystoscopy, ureteric catheterization, retrograde pyelogram
clinical_indications: To help in reducing renal colic or to allow drainage when infection is present or when a stone prevents urine flow adequately.
Stents are commonly placed after surgery for stones (ureteroscopy) to allow healing and to ensure that swelling does not block the drainage of urine after the procedure.

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management
CT-IVP/ IVP confirming the diagnosis and the need of this surgery

claim.label: Detailed Procedure / operation notes detailing findings of cystoscopy/ Retrograde pyelogram and ureteric catheterization

ppd_preauth.q: Are the clinical notes and CT-IVP/ IVP report indicative of DJ Stenting procedure?

common_queries: Clinical notes or CT-IVP/IVP report not indicative of DJ Stenting procedure.
CT-IVP/ IVP confirming diagnosis and need for surgery not submitted at pre-authorization stage.
Post Procedure x-ray showing stent not submitted at claim stage.
Detailed Procedure / operation notes detailing findings of cystoscopy, retrograde pyelogram and ureteric catheterization not submitted at claim stage.

clinical_key_pointers: Ureteral stents are placed temporarily into the ureter to drain urine from the kidney into bladder. The stent allows drainage around a stone or can speed up healing after stone surgery.
A double-J stent is a ureteral stent with curving ends that prevent the stent slipping into the bladder or the kidney. Usually after several days or few weeks these stents will be removed.
